title: tordylium absinthiifolium
intro: Tordylium absinthiifolium, also known as Wild Rampion or Cuckoo Flower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, including vitamin C, potassium, and iron, which provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. Tordylium absinthiifolium can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies and traditional medicine.
